project__comments.comments.answer:
Pre-renovation hazardous materials survey for former light-industrial warehouse being converted to mixed-use commercial space. Client intends to begin selective demolition of interior partitions, removal of old boiler system, and replacement of original 1950s roofing system within the next 60–90 days.

Scope of sampling for this COC:
- Suspect asbestos-containing materials (ACM) including pipe insulation, elbow fittings, floor tile and mastic, boiler insulation, and roofing materials.
- Limited lead paint chip sampling from original steel window frames and interior CMU walls in loading dock area.

Site conditions and access details:
- Building is currently vacant and secured; power is available only in the north electrical room. All samples collected under Level C PPE with half-face respirators and P100 cartridges. Airborne dust was minimal during sampling; no active construction activities at time of survey.
- Areas sampled include: basement mechanical room, first-floor warehouse, second-floor office mezzanine, and roof level. Roof was dry at time of sampling; no standing water observed.

Special instructions for laboratory:
- Please prioritize asbestos PLM analysis for boiler insulation (Samples L-06 and L-07) and roof core (Sample L-10), as these results are needed for abatement planning and project budgeting meetings scheduled for 2026-03-05.
- If any friable thermal system insulation (TSI) is found to be non-detect by standard PLM but visually appears fibrous, please flag those samples in the report so the client can consider TEM confirmation if needed.
- For lead paint chip samples (L-08 and L-09), please report results in mg/kg and indicate whether each result is above or below the 1.0 mg/cm² or 0.5% by weight regulatory thresholds (if applicable under Illinois and federal HUD/EPA guidance).

Reporting and contacts:
- Send electronic PDF report to: mthompson@thompson-pm.com (client) and cc: lchen@midwest-enviroconsulting.com (consultant). No hard copy required.
- If any sample requires re-collection or clarification, please contact Laura Chen directly by phone at (312) 555-7310 during business hours (8:00 AM–5:00 PM CT).

Additional notes:
- Chain-of-custody maintained continuously by Laura Chen from time of sampling until delivery at ACM Engineering & Environmental Services, Inc. laboratory front desk. Samples were transported in a rigid cooler with ice packs to maintain stable temperatures and prevent degradation.
- All sample containers are labeled with waterproof ink using the sample IDs listed below (L-01 through L-10). Each sample is double-bagged in zip-top bags to minimize cross-contamination. No visible damage or leakage observed upon drop-off.

sample_table.sample_table[0].sample_desc.answer: Basement mechanical room – 2" pipe insulation, white fibrous wrap, Sample 1 of 2

sample_table.sample_table[0].sample_loca.answer: Basement mechanical room, along east wall near condensate tank

sample_table.sample_table[0].area_volume.answer: Approx. 6 linear feet of 2" diameter pipe; grab sample of outer wrap and underlying insulation

sample_table.sample_table[1].sample_desc.answer: Basement mechanical room – 2" pipe insulation, white fibrous wrap, Sample 2 of 2 (confirmatory)

sample_table.sample_table[1].sample_loca.answer: Basement mechanical room, west wall return line near floor drain

sample_table.sample_table[1].area_volume.answer: Approx. 4 linear feet of 2" diameter pipe; grab sample of outer wrap and underlying insulation

sample_table.sample_table[2].sample_desc.answer: First-floor warehouse – 9" x 9" brown floor tile with black mastic

sample_table.sample_table[2].sample_loca.answer: First-floor central warehouse bay, gridline C-4

sample_table.sample_table[2].area_volume.answer: Approx. 1,200 sq ft of 9" x 9" VAT; composite of 3 tiles plus underlying mastic

sample_table.sample_table[3].sample_desc.answer: First-floor warehouse – 9" x 9" beige floor tile with tan mastic (visually distinct from L-03)

sample_table.sample_table[3].sample_loca.answer: First-floor warehouse, northwest corner near former office partition

sample_table.sample_table[3].area_volume.answer: Approx. 600 sq ft of 9" x 9" VAT; composite of 3 tiles plus underlying mastic

sample_table.sample_table[4].sample_desc.answer: Second-floor office mezzanine – gypsum wallboard with joint compound

sample_table.sample_table[4].sample_loca.answer: Second-floor office corridor, south wall between Rooms 201 and 202

sample_table.sample_table[4].area_volume.answer: Approx. 300 sq ft of gypsum board partition; composite of wallboard and joint compound from 3 locations

sample_table.sample_table[5].sample_desc.answer: Boiler room – boiler exterior insulation, white fibrous material

sample_table.sample_table[5].sample_loca.answer: Basement boiler room, main boiler south face

sample_table.sample_table[5].area_volume.answer: Approx. 80 sq ft of boiler exterior insulation; grab sample of outer layer and underlying fibrous material

sample_table.sample_table[6].sample_desc.answer: Boiler room – fitting insulation on 90° elbow, white/grey plaster-like material

sample_table.sample_table[6].sample_loca.answer: Basement boiler room, overhead 3" steam line elbow above boiler platform

sample_table.sample_table[6].area_volume.answer: Single elbow insulation, approx. 8" diameter; small grab sample from intact fitting

sample_table.sample_table[7].sample_desc.answer: Lead paint chip – original steel window frame, exterior side, dark green coating

sample_table.sample_table[7].sample_loca.answer: First-floor south elevation, center bay steel window frame

sample_table.sample_table[7].area_volume.answer: Approx. 4 sq in of paint chips from multiple locations on same window frame

sample_table.sample_table[8].sample_desc.answer: Lead paint chip – interior CMU wall, light grey paint, loading dock area

sample_table.sample_table[8].sample_loca.answer: First-floor loading dock, east wall near overhead door

sample_table.sample_table[8].area_volume.answer: Approx. 3 sq in of paint chips from lower wall (2–4 ft above floor)

sample_table.sample_table[9].sample_desc.answer: Roof core – built-up roofing system with gravel surfacing

sample_table.sample_table[9].sample_loca.answer: Main roof, approximately 15 ft north of south parapet and 20 ft east of west parapet

sample_table.sample_table[9].area_volume.answer: Roof core approx. 3" diameter through gravel, bituminous layers, and insulation down to deck
